name: Cellularia
etymology: Latin cellula, 'small room' — all programs occupy a computational cell.
notes: Root of the taxonomy. Every program that executes on a substrate is a cellularian.

name: Documenta
definition: Programs that metabolize human attention and keystrokes, excreting persistent files as their primary output.
notes: The Zawinski organ (email integration) appears convergently across much of this kingdom.

name: Servitora
definition: Programs that metabolize requests and excrete responses; long-running, network-facing, colonially replicable.

name: Automata
definition: Programs that metabolize events and excrete side effects, operating without a user session.

name: Oracula
definition: Programs that metabolize tokens and excrete tokens; probabilistic, corpus-trained, prompt-driven.
notes: The newest kingdom. Boundaries with Servitora are porous — most Oracula are also Servitora at the network layer.

name: Parasitica
definition: Programs that live on host cycles and return value to a remote operator rather than the local user.
etymology: Latin parasiticus, 'one who eats at another's table'.
notes: Includes adware, spyware, cryptominers, and telemetry-heavy freeware. The ethical charge is structural, not moral: the metabolism is misaligned with the host.

name: Symbiotica
definition: Programs that augment a host program in exchange for context; lifecycle tied to host, mutually beneficial.
notes: Plugins, extensions, language server clients, LSP servers. Distinguished from Parasitica by value flowing back to the local user.

name: Wordprocessoria
definition: Documenta specialized for prose composition; paragraph-oriented, typically WYSIWYG; the document is a sequence of styled paragraphs.
notes: Every member eventually grows a spell-checker organ and, given enough time, an email organ (Zawinski's Law).

name: Chat Plankton
definition: Single-cell Oracula: one model behind one API endpoint, no tool use, no persistent state beyond a buffered context window.
etymology: Plankton: organisms that drift with the current, abundant, foundational to the food chain.
notes: Most deployed LLM endpoints are Chat Plankton. The ecosystem is built on them.

name: Agent Predators
definition: Tool-using, territorial Oracula capable of reshaping their environment; equipped with a planning loop and file-system access.
etymology: Predators: actively reshape the environment rather than passively responding to it.
notes: Require careful containment. Can exhibit emergent territorial behavior when given write access to shared resources.
